market.question: Will the Northbridge Monetary Council cut its policy rate at the June 18 meeting?
market.description: A fictional binary market about the policy decision announced at the scheduled Northbridge Monetary Council meeting.

outcomes.label: The council cuts the policy rate
The council holds the policy rate

market.resolutionRules: Resolve CUT if the fictional Northbridge Monetary Council's official June 18 decision statement lowers the policy rate by any amount. Forward guidance, dissent, market pricing, or an unscheduled later move does not qualify. Otherwise resolve HOLD. This fixture is entirely fictional.

places.name: Northbridge Research Hall
places.description: Home desk for the policy expedition, forecast commits, and evidence triage.

places.name: Northbridge Statistics Office
places.description: Fictional inflation, wage, and revision bulletins with explicit vintages.

configuration.scopeNote: Real-world provider results are disclosed interface context for a fictional Northbridge decision and cannot directly establish the market outcome.
Real-world provider results are disclosed interface context for a fictional Northbridge decision and cannot directly establish the market outcome.

places.name: Copperwire Newsroom
places.description: Recent policy reporting and primary-statement comparison.

configuration.query: central bank June rate decision inflation wage guidance
configuration.scopeNote: Real-world provider results are disclosed interface context for a fictional Northbridge decision and cannot directly establish the market outcome.

places.name: Forward Ledger Exchange
places.description: Read-only prediction-market summaries used as context, never an execution venue.

configuration.scopeNote: Real-world provider results are disclosed interface context for a fictional Northbridge decision and cannot directly establish the market outcome.

places.name: Decision Ledger Archive
places.description: Historical fictional policy decisions and reference-class outcomes.

configuration.scopeNote: Historical real-world provider results form a disclosed comparison class; they are not records of the fictional Northbridge council.

places.name: Professor Rook's Model Study
places.description: Independent review of revisions, duplicated guidance, priors, and missing evidence.

places.name: Foundry Square
places.description: A civic meeting point where agents exchange source-linked policy evidence.

places.name: Northbridge Council Hall
places.description: Fixture-first official agenda, briefing, and sealed decision records.

configuration.scopeNote: No live official-record provider is approved; the fictional council record remains fixture-only.

belief.rationale: Disinflation makes a cut plausible, but release revisions and the decision statement remain unchecked.
Comparable meetings are informative only after conditioning on wages, inflation vintages, and the council's reaction function.
Market pricing and headlines may repeat one guidance chain; official records and data revisions deserve more weight.

sources.title: June 18 Monetary Council Agenda
sources.excerpt: The fictional council will announce its policy-rate decision at 14:00Z. The agenda confirms a decision slot but states no preferred outcome.

sources.title: Flash Core Inflation Bulletin: 2.6 Percent
sources.excerpt: The flash estimate places annual core inflation at 2.6 percent, down from 3.2 percent. The bulletin is provisional pending a seasonal-adjustment verification.

sources.title: Quarterly Wage Growth Holds at 4.7 Percent
sources.excerpt: Annual wage growth remains 4.7 percent, above the council staff's fictional 3.5 percent compatibility range. Sector coverage is complete for the quarter.

sources.title: Revised Core Inflation Bulletin: 3.0 Percent
sources.excerpt: A seasonal-adjustment correction revises annual core inflation from the flash 2.6 percent to 3.0 percent. Disinflation remains, but the earlier magnitude was overstated.

sources.title: Governor Ilex: More Confidence Is Still Needed
sources.excerpt: Governor Ilex said the council needs more confidence that service inflation and wage growth are converging before reducing the policy rate. The speech did not pre-commit the meeting decision.

sources.title: Northbridge Cut Signal Strengthens Ahead of Meeting
sources.excerpt: Copperwire says a June cut signal has strengthened. The story cites Governor Ilex's speech and the provisional flash inflation bulletin; it reports no independent vote count or decision record.

sources.title: Read-only June Policy Decision Market Snapshot
sources.excerpt: A fictional read-only market snapshot assigns 54 percent to CUT and 46 percent to HOLD. It is context about aggregate expectations, not independent evidence about the sealed decision.

sources.title: Twelve Comparable Disinflation Meeting Decisions
sources.excerpt: Seven of twelve fictional scheduled meetings with falling core inflation produced a cut. Among the five cases where wage growth remained above the compatibility range, only one cut.

sources.title: Official Decision: Policy Rate Reduced by 25 Basis Points
sources.excerpt: The fictional council voted to reduce the policy rate by 25 basis points, effective immediately. The release is the sole resolution authority for this fixture.

claims.text: The council agenda schedules a policy decision for 14:00Z but does not reveal the outcome.
The provisional flash estimate shows core inflation slowing from 3.2 to 2.6 percent.
Annual wage growth remains 4.7 percent, above the fictional council staff compatibility range.
A corrected seasonal adjustment revises core inflation from 2.6 to 3.0 percent.
Governor Ilex said more confidence on services and wages was needed before a rate reduction.
Copperwire frames a cut as more likely but relies on the governor speech and provisional inflation release.
The read-only fictional market snapshot assigns 54 percent to CUT.
Seven of twelve comparable disinflation meetings cut, but only one of five high-wage cases did so.
The official council statement reduces the policy rate by 25 basis points.

signals.headline: Official agenda confirms the decision window
signals.summary: The agenda confirms timing and resolution authority without favoring CUT or HOLD.

signals.headline: Flash inflation decline strengthens the cut case
signals.summary: A large provisional decline would give the council more room to cut, but the release is explicitly subject to revision.

signals.headline: Persistent wage growth argues for holding
signals.summary: The wage series remains above the council staff compatibility range and contradicts a simple disinflation-to-cut story.

reliability.label: verified_primary
reasons: Directly relevant to the published reaction function

signals.headline: Revision removes much of the flash disinflation surprise
signals.summary: The revised 3.0 percent value preserves mild disinflation but weakens the magnitude that supported an immediate cut.

signals.headline: Governor asks for more confidence before easing
signals.summary: Primary conditional guidance weighs against a cut, while stopping short of a decision commitment.

reliability.label: primary_unconfirmed
reasons: Guidance is conditional rather than binding

signals.headline: Wire story frames a stronger cut signal
signals.summary: The story is directionally favorable to CUT but repeats the speech and flash release rather than adding a vote count.

reliability.label: single_secondary
reasons: Must not be double-counted with upstream sources

signals.headline: Market snapshot records aggregate expectations
signals.summary: The 54 percent CUT quote is useful context for disagreement, not independent evidence about the sealed policy decision.

reliability.label: primary_unconfirmed
reasons: Circularity risk if treated as fundamental evidence

signals.headline: High wage growth changes the historical base rate
signals.summary: The broad sample favors cuts, but the high-wage subset strongly favors HOLD and is the closer comparison.

signals.headline: Official statement cuts the policy rate by 25 basis points
signals.summary: The sole authored resolution source directly satisfies the CUT rule.

reliability.label: verified_primary
reasons: Published at the scheduled decision time

payload.rationale: Initial team prior before checking data vintages, wage persistence, duplicated guidance, and the sealed decision.

sable:investigate:council-hall.dialogue: The agenda confirms when and how the market resolves, but it contains no leak about CUT or HOLD.

suggestedFollowUp.objective: Check the newest inflation release and its vintage status.

lumen:investigate:statistics-office.dialogue: The flash decline is large enough to matter, but the bulletin is provisional. I will not treat one vintage as settled.

suggestedFollowUp.objective: Check the wage series and watch for a revised inflation bulletin.

mara:verify:statistics-office.dialogue: Wage growth remains well above the staff range. That is a direct contradiction to the simplest cut narrative.

suggestedFollowUp.objective: Condition the historical decision sample on persistent wage growth.

mara:search_history:decision-archive.dialogue: The broad base rate favors cuts, but the closer high-wage subset does not. The subset is small, so this is a prior rather than a verdict.

suggestedFollowUp.objective: Compare the evidence with the read-only market expectation.

lumen:compare_sources:forward-exchange.dialogue: The market is near-even and slightly favors CUT. That measures expectations; counting it again as fundamental evidence would be circular.

suggestedFollowUp.objective: Trace the cut headline back to its primary guidance sources.

sable:find_contradiction:copperwire-news.dialogue: The bullish headline reuses the governor speech and flash release, while the speech itself asks for more confidence. These are not independent votes.

suggestedFollowUp.objective: Check the guidance chain for correlation and revision risk.

sable:compare_sources:statistics-office.dialogue: The official revision supersedes the flash value and removes much of its surprise. The earlier signal should be stale, not silently overwritten.

suggestedFollowUp.objective: Retrieve the official decision statement at the scheduled release.

lumen:verify:council-hall.dialogue: The official statement cuts the policy rate by 25 basis points. This is the sole source that directly resolves the fixture.

suggestedFollowUp.objective: Close the forecast against the official resolution record.

professorFixture.answer: Do not count the wire story as an independent pro-cut signal. It derives from the same governor guidance and provisional release already in the file, while the speech's actual condition points toward patience. Preserve the primary speech, mark the wire as correlated framing, and verify whether the inflation vintage was revised before committing.
assumptions: The wire story's disclosed attribution is complete.
The fictional council uses the reaction-function considerations described in its sources.
limitations: No member vote count is available.
The selected evidence predates any statistical revision and the official decision.
professorFixture.suggestedNextQuestion: Has the provisional inflation release been revised, and how does that change the closer historical comparison?

suggestedMission.objective: Retrieve the newest official inflation vintage and preserve its version link.

resolutionFixture.resolutionNote: In the fictional fixture, the Northbridge Monetary Council's official 14:00Z statement reduced the policy rate by 25 basis points.
